question_structure.context: The AC generator at the power plant outputs a constant voltage of $200\mathrm{V}$ and a power of $100\mathrm{kW}$. The electricity needs to be transmitted to a distant factory. The designed power loss on the transmission line is $10\%$, and the total resistance of the transmission line is $100\Omega$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: What is the current on the transmission line?
question_structure.sub_question_2: What is the turns ratio of the step-up transformer required for power transmission (assuming the transformer used is an ideal transformer)?

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the problem, the power loss on the transmission line is: $\Delta P = P_{out} \times 10\% = 10 \mathrm{kW}$

sub_question_2.step_4: According to the principle of transformers, the turns ratio of the step-up transformer required for power transmission is $n_{1} : n_{2} = I_{2} : I_{1} = 10 : 500 = 1 : 50$.

result_quantity.name: Power loss on the transmission line

step_4.physical_theorem: Transformer turns ratio and current relationship

result_quantity.name: Turns ratio of the step-up transformer
